untranslatable_reason: Signal requires meta-level pattern validation ('Pattern has occurred at least 10 times in historical data', 'Win rate of pattern ≥ 55% across all 10+ occurrences', 'Current market setup matches pattern definition'). These conditions require pattern recognition logic, historical backtesting statistics, and user-defined pattern matching that cannot be expressed using only the available technical indicators and operators. The signal is a behavioral/bias-avoidance rule requiring custom pattern-tracking and win-rate calculation logic at the backtest engine level, not translatable to indicator-based conditions.
translation_notes: This signal from Van Tharp addresses the law of small numbers bias by requiring statistical validation of patterns (minimum 10 occurrences, 55%+ win rate) before entry. The entry/exit conditions are placeholder values. Implementation requires: (1) a pattern registry with historical occurrence tracking, (2) win-rate calculation per pattern, and (3) pattern-matching logic. The 1.5× ATR stop-loss and 2× ATR profit target reference atr_14 or atr_20 but require dynamic ATR multiplier functionality not present in basic condition operators. Recommend implementing as a custom pre-filter module before standard backtest signal conditions.
